text: Hi, my name is Crystal and this is Rocky and this is Four Muddy Claws. I've been grooming for a little over 16 years.
I'm Christy and I'm also a groomer at Four Muddy Claws in St. Louis.
I love it. It's really relaxing and fun to work with the dogs and it's a challenge. It's something different every day.
Some people think you need to be well endowed in the front to hold this dryer, but you don't, obviously.
So, it just sticks in your t-shirt and you just keep it on low and either a warm or a cool setting. It depends on your dog.
It helps keep the hair fluffed up so that you can scissor it better.
I've got a straight blade, a curved blade, and I've got my barber's comb and my scissor brush.
Big dogs, small dogs. Which is easiest?
Probably big dogs because they're just a little sturdier on the table. They don't fly around.
Do you have any diva dogs?
Oh yeah, tons of diva dogs.
Any hazards of the dog?
Yeah, I mean you can always get bit or scratched, but mostly we're worried about the safety of the dog.
Of course, they have a risk of getting scratched themselves.
We try to do it on our own.
We do everything we can to minimize that risk.
What you can do at home is just to keep their hair brushed out and, you know, with a brush, a slicker brush,
and then a comb is nice to get, you know, make sure you've got all the tangles out.
I don't recommend bathing more than once a month on most dogs.
Are you happy, Rocky?
